response:
The quadratic equation for the batting score is S = 50 + 5n - 0.2n^2.
To find the maximum score we locate the vertex of this parabola.
The vertex occurs at n = -b / (2a) where b is 5 and a is -0.2.
Calculating the value gives n = -5 / (2 * -0.2) which equals 12.5.
Since practice sessions must be whole numbers we check n = 12 and n = 13.
At n = 12 the score S is 50 + 60 - 28.8 resulting in 81.2.
At n = 13 the score S is 50 + 65 - 33.8 resulting in 81.2.
Both values yield the same maximum average batting score of 81.2.
Now we calculate the win probability for the two scenarios requested.
First we find the score when n is 5 sessions per month.
Substituting 5 into the equation gives S = 50 + 25 - 5 equaling 70.
Next we calculate the score when n is 10 sessions per month.
Substituting 10 into the equation gives S = 50 + 50 - 20 equaling 80.
We now apply the logistic function P = 1 / (1 + e^-(0.1S - 3)).
For the first case with S = 70 the exponent is -(7 - 3) which is -4.
This results in a probability of 1 / (1 + e^-4) approximately 0.9820.
For the second case with S = 80 the exponent is -(8 - 3) which is -5.
This results in a probability of 1 / (1 + e^-5) approximately 0.9933.
The change in win probability is the difference between these two values.
Subtracting 0.9820 from 0.9933 gives a positive increase of 0.0113.
The team improves their chances of winning by about 1.13 percent.
This strategic adjustment demonstrates the value of consistent training for overall health.
